question: Explain what JavaScript BOM (Browser Object Model) is and why it matters in JavaScript.
answer: JavaScript BOM (Browser Object Model) is Explore the Browser Object Model: window, navigator, screen, location, history, and timing events for browser interaction. It matters because it helps developers write cleaner, more maintainable code and is fundamental to modern JavaScript development.

question: What are the key concepts of JavaScript BOM (Browser Object Model)?
answer: The key concepts include: window object, navigator object (userAgent, geolocation), screen object, location object, history object (back, forward, pushState), setTimeout and setInterval. Mastering these will give you a solid foundation in javascript bom (browser object model).
